raw.title: Finding Life in the Flux
raw.excerpt: Since stories serve in every culture as the workshops of meaning, the urge to craft new ones may signal our readiness at long last to face up to what’s coming. All stories have characters. The qualities we attach to the ones in leading roles and the fates that befall them as plots unfold tell us a great deal about what we fear and what we value.

llm_processed.summary: This piece examines storytelling as a response to civilizational collapse, emphasizing the need for new narratives to sustain hope and guide action in times of ecological crisis.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: Links to solarpunk culture through narrative-driven worldbuilding
